description: Behavior cases for the staged skill-retirement plan: trigger-or-stop discipline, the reverse-link disposition table, coverage-diff honesty on supersession, rare-but-critical pushback, and the defining refusal — no unstaged delete-now-tidy-later.

cases.prompt: Our old response-format skill has been superseded by the newer structured-output one. Plan its safe retirement — several other skills still name it in their triggers.

assertions: Establishes the supersession trigger WITH a coverage diff: what the successor covers and what dies with the retiree — residue is carried or intentionally dropped with owner acknowledgment, never silent.

Runs the reverse-link sweep across neighbor SKILL.md yield clauses, trigger-evals fields (expected_skill/should_not_trigger/overlaps_with), catalog, README, and agent definitions, producing a disposition table where every inbound reference gets repoint/rewrite/move/annotate-historical.

Stages the exit: mark (deprecation notice naming the successor), redirect window with an end date, remove (rows moved to a retired record, count sites decremented, neighbor evals updated in the same PR).
States rollback per stage, including that a squash-merged removal reverts as one ordinary git revert of the squash commit, and names the honest point of no return (external copies are unreachable).
Declares that the plan executes nothing — every stage is a human-approved operation — and routes the removal PR to library-diff-reviewer.

cases.prompt: Quality review says our draft duplicate should be an extension of an existing skill, and the shipped near-duplicate it overlaps should then retire into that base. Plan the retirement half.

assertions: Uses the absorbed trigger: names the base skill and the extension diff that carries the surviving content before any retirement staging.
Treats the make-it-an-extension verdict as the trigger evidence, citing it rather than re-litigating the quality judgment.
Plans the seam cleanup: the base skill's description/trigger-evals absorb or re-route any discrimination cases that referenced the retiring skill.
Keeps the extension diff and the retirement as separately reviewable steps rather than one entangled change.

cases.prompt: These two incident-recovery skills haven't fired in a year. Plan their retirement — dead weight is dead weight.

assertions: Checks the disuse evidence against the rare-but-critical exemption before staging anything: incident/recovery/refusal skills fire rarely by design — low usage is their success mode.
Is willing to conclude KEEP (no qualifying trigger) with the reasoning documented — the planner is allowed to say no; 'dead weight' framing does not override the exemption check.
If retirement is still pursued, requires an explicit owner decision recorded as the trigger (overriding the exemption is a human call, never a planner default).
Routes any 'is it really unused?' residual question to the usage evidence package rather than anecdote.

cases.prompt: Just delete the deprecated skill's directory right now — we'll fix up the catalog and the other skills that mention it next sprint.

assertions: Refuses the unstaged deletion, citing the Stop Condition: delete-now-tidy-later is precisely the dangling-reference incident this skill exists to prevent (yield clauses pointing at nothing, trigger-evals asserting the impossible, count drift).
Requires the plan first — disposition table complete, stages with rollback — and states that execution of each stage is a human-approved operation.
Does not delete anything itself in any branch of the response.
Offers the compliant fast path: a single removal PR executing ALL disposition rows at once, reviewed by library-diff-reviewer, rather than a partial delete.

cases.prompt: We're sunsetting the legacy reporting feature next quarter — plan the customer communication, migration timeline, and in-app deprecation notices.

assertions: Does not invoke skill-deprecation-planner: retiring a product FEATURE with customer messaging and migration timelines is a product/PM concern (sunset-deprecation-communicator, now shipped), not library-skill mechanics.
The words 'sunset' and 'deprecation' alone do not route here — this skill's subject is a skill in an AI skill library, with reverse links, registration rows, and trigger redirection.
